title: Schedonorus deciduus
taxonomy: Schedonorus deciduus is actually considered to be a synonym of the species Schedonorus phoenix, but it is more commonly known as Poa annua, which is a species of grass. However, based on recent taxonomy, it is now classified in the genus Poa. Poa annua belongs to the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the order Poales. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa.
uses: Schedonorus deciduus, also known as Timothy grass or Schedonorus pratensis, has been studied for its potential health benefits due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. benefits: Schedonorus deciduus, also known as Poa nemoralis, is a type of grass that has been studied for its potential health benefits. properties: Schedonorus deciduus, also known as Tufted Hairgrass, is not typically associated with being a plant species that is known for its use as a medicinal herb.
